company.name: Pilin' It Higher Farms
company.instagram_bio: Tier 2 Cannabis Cultivator perched high in the hills of Duxbury, VT. Greenhouse Grown on a Solar Powered off-grid farm.
tagline_options: Grown with roots. Raised on rhythm.

background: Bassist for Vermont jam band The Edd
Legacy cannabis cultivator with decades of experience
Lifelong Vermont resident, raised in Dover
Vegetable and cannabis grower by nature
greg_domina.voice_quote: I love growing stuff. I've always been growing vegetables and cannabis anyway.

background: Co-owner and partner in Pilin' It Higher Farms
Guitarist — can handle himself with a guitar in his hands
Extensive experience in the relatively new Vermont cannabis industry (legacy grower)
Part of the music and growing community that built this brand

brand_story.name_origin: Named after a Disco Biscuits song — reflects the founders' deep roots in jam band culture
brand_story.ethos: A legacy grower going legit. Deep Vermont roots, music-soaked soul, craft-first growing philosophy.
brand_story.journey: Started legalization process January 2023, received CCB approval April 2024. A slow, deliberate build.

strains.name: Snowberry
strains.description: Decade-old mother plant. Bred for Vermont — mold-resistant, thrives in the local climate. A true heirloom.

strains.name: Zerbtane
strains.description: Highest THC offering. Oregon-bred genetics brought to Vermont soil.

strains.name: Amnesia Daze
strains.description: Sativa-leaning, cerebral, citrus-forward. Opens things up.

strains.name: Mintz Royale
strains.description: Cool and creamy up front, serious underneath. Mint and sweet earth.

strains.name: Diesel Dough
strains.description: Diesel genetics meeting Vermont living soil. Fuel and sweetness, complex and pungent.

cultivation.notes: Year-round growing capability. Weather-protected. Solar-powered off-grid. Living soil methodology. Hands-on, small-batch philosophy.

personality: Authentic — no hype, no corporate gloss
Vermont-proud — seasons, soil, small-town craft, off-grid living
Technically fluent — IPM, living soil, terpene profiles — but never gatekeeping
Understated confidence — they know their craft, they don't need to shout
Warmly irreverent — humor welcome, never crass
voice_examples_real: When the fruits of your labor look, smell, & taste this good it's safe to say you're doing it right 🤙 Proud to be back at it again with our 3rd harvest bringing quality off-grid/living soil/greenhouse cannabis to the good people of VT!!

Trimming up some Snowberry and this strain never ceases to amaze me. Gloves are mighty sticky and it reeks like pine infused jet fuel ⛽️
If you've smoked it then you know why we've kept the cut for so many years! Here's to a lot more of it in 2025.

Part of a healthy grow is having some solid IPM practices! Yesterday we released 5,000 minute pirate bugs and 10,000 green lacewing into our greenhouses. These guys devour pests and we thank them for their hard work!

Almost time to get everyone into their forever homes! Growing up in Southern VT I'd always plant mid May but Duxbury is a different world! First week of June seems to be the sweet spot on our property.

tone_by_channel.instagram: Conversational, visual storytelling, music references natural. Hashtags: minimal and purposeful.
tone_by_channel.facebook: Community-oriented, slightly longer form, good for event/retail announcements.
tone_by_channel.website: Warm and narrative. Tell the story. Let the product speak.
tone_by_channel.press: Grounded, factual, founder-voice. Let Greg's authenticity carry it.

do: Reference Vermont seasons, landscape, and culture
Weave in music references naturally — Disco Biscuits, jam bands, The Edd
Center Greg and Danielle's story and craft
Use plain, vivid language — no cannabis industry jargon
Highlight the legacy-to-legal journey as a point of pride
dont: Use exaggerated cannabis slang ('dank', 'fire', 'loud') unless used ironically
Make medical claims or imply health benefits
Use generic 'cannabis lifestyle' tropes (people getting stoned on couches)

visual_identity.mood: Think: a Phish poster designed by a Vermont farmer. Psychedelic undertones kept tasteful.
visual_identity.photography_style: Real — actual greenhouse, actual plants, actual people. No stock. Seasonal.

audience.primary: Vermont cannabis consumers who value craft and provenance
audience.secondary: Jam band / music community in Vermont and New England
audience.tertiary: Legacy cannabis culture looking for trusted, known growers going legit

legal_compliance_notes: Vermont Adult Use cannabis — all content must comply with Vermont Cannabis Control Board advertising rules
No marketing to minors — no imagery or language that could appeal to under-21 audiences
Include required disclosures on all advertising materials
